text:
Once there was a wise old owl named Hoots. Every dawn, Hoots would flutter down from his nest in the ancient oak and stretch his big Wise Owl eyes into the brightness of sunrise. He would then proceed to scan the world, ensuring everything was right for another day.

One particular day, as Hoots perched on top of the stone owl statue, he noticed something fishy. A shiny green scale caught his attention. Curiosity led him to notice more. A minuscule green dragon slithered across the leaf below. "Mmmm," Hoots murmured knowingly.

In no time, a pair of golden-eyed pixies fluttered down from the branches to investigate this mystery. "What's going on here?" their leader asked with tiny giggles.

Hoots said, "Oh, just a little dragon in distress."

The pixies giggled among themselves. "Dragon?"

"One of their scales fell off," explained Hoots.
